summary: Recent updates to the negotiation engine in Firefox's WebRTC implementation have focused on improving memory management and enhancing codec handling. By transitioning to **`UniquePtr`**, a smart pointer that helps manage resource ownership automatically, Firefox can reduce potential memory leaks and improve performance when cloning codec and session descriptions in WebRTC, the technology behind video calls. Additionally, these changes include handling certain codec configurations more gracefully, such as avoiding errors related to missing retransmission (RTX) payload types. These updates not only streamline the code but also ensure a more robust and efficient video call experience for users.
